title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Network Control Room
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a layered packet-flow diagnosis while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.
